article_meta.title: The three-way battle between Democrats running for Senate in Illinois has evolved into an argument about the best way to

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language, attributes contentious phrases to the candidates, and avoids adopting the article’s more charged framings. It includes multiple perspectives present in the article and refrains from sensational or subjective wording.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and factual descriptions; charged phrases are attributed to candidates.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims about political risk and recent events are grounded in the article’s content.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Subjective judgments are framed as party debates or concerns, not asserted by the model.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language beyond factual incidents.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No un-attributed inferences about motives or intentions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Covers all three candidates’ positions and internal party concerns; slight emphasis on anti-ICE framing consistent with article focus.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes key perspectives (candidates, intra-party concerns, related events).
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced depiction of risks and political appeal of the stance.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege elite or grassroots voices disproportionately.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source; avoids loaded terminology except when attributing to speakers.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents candidates, intra-party strategists’ concerns, and contextual events proportionally to the article.
